title: YSK: Nerve-sparing mastectomies that let you feel your restored breasts (or flat chest) are a real thing.
body:
Many women get a mastectomy and expect to lose sensation (or worse, aren't told to expect it, but discover they are numb afterward). For many years, this has been part of the package with a mastectomy and one of the sacrifices we just had to accept.


But things are changing! Over the last few years, some surgeons are practicing nerve-sparing and/or nerve-restoring mastectomies. They either attempt not to cut the nerves in the first place or do nerve-grafting (nerve neurotization), to try and repair cut nerves. 80 or 90% of the time this gives the woman at least SOME sensation post-mastectomy. This can be done with implant-based or DIEP flap reconstruction, as well as flat closures or goldilocks. 


Your local breast surgeon or the first surgeon you talk to may not be familiar with this idea and may even tell you it's not possible or is a scam/myth. Please don't believe them. It's real, it's just new. Facebook (I know, Facebook, ugh), is a great resource to connect with other women who have feeling after their DMX. A group called The Peled Girls is dedicated to women who had their DMX with Dr. Anne Peled, a surgeon who is pioneering this type of mastectomy. Nearly all of them have feeling in their breasts after implant-based reconstruction.


Resensation.com has SOME of the surgeons who offer nerve grafting, but the directory there is not a complete list. Your local or state-wide FB breast cancer support group may be able to recommend a surgeon who has experience doing sensation-sparing mastectomies. That is how I found a surgeon in my own state.


I just want to put this information out there because so many women aren't advised of this possibility.
